content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


January 26, 2005

Martin Baldwin
900 Sunset Circle 18
Crescent City, CA 95531

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-006

Dear Mr. Baldwin:

This letter confirms the receipt of your January 18, 2005, request for advice regarding the appointment of Herb Kolodner to the Crescent City Council. While the Commission may provide informal assistance (see regulation 18329(c)(1)), you do not identify the person or persons you are authorized to represent. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329, unless you are an authorized representative of the person for whom you seek assistance, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B) and (c)(1).) Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

Your inquiry also presents questions outside the purview of the Political Reform Act. Section 81002 provides the parameters of the Fair Political Practices Commission's jurisdiction:

"§ 81002. Purposes of Title.
The people enact this title to accomplish the following purposes:
(a) Receipts and expenditures in election campaigns should be fully and truthfully disclosed in order that the voters may be fully informed and improper practices may be inhibited.
(b) The activities of lobbyists should be regulated and their finances disclosed in order that improper influences will not be directed at public officials.
(c) Assets and income of public officials which may be materially affected by their official actions should be disclosed and in appropriate circumstances the officials should be disqualified from acting in order that conflicts of interest may be avoided.
(d) The state ballot pamphlet should be converted into a useful document so that voters will not be entirely dependent on paid advertising for information regarding state measures.
(e) Laws and practices unfairly favoring incumbents should be abolished in order that elections may be conducted more fairly.
(f) Adequate enforcement mechanisms should be provided to public officials and private citizens in order that this title will be vigorously enforced."

Advice Letter No. W-05-006
Page No. 2

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Enclosure

JWW:jg

sections.conclusion: The request is withdrawn because the requester did not identify the person or persons he is authorized to represent, which is required under California Code of Regulations 18329. Additionally, the inquiry presents questions outside the purview of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Martin Baldwin requested advice regarding the appointment of Herb Kolodner to the Crescent City Council, but did not identify the person or persons he was authorized to represent.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request because the requester failed to establish that he was an authorized representative of the person for whom advice was sought, and the inquiry also fell outside the Commission's jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (W-05-006) rather than a standard advice letter. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections present, but the factual basis for withdrawal and jurisdictional analysis are clearly stated in the body of the letter.

QUESTION: Martin Baldwin requested advice regarding the appointment of Herb Kolodner to the Crescent City Council, but did not identify the person or persons he was authorized to represent.

CONCLUSION: The request is withdrawn because the requester did not identify the person or persons he is authorized to represent, which is required under California Code of Regulations 18329. Additionally, the inquiry presents questions outside the purview of the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC withdrew Martin Baldwin's January 18, 2005 request for advice regarding a Crescent City Council appointment because he did not identify whom he was authorized to represent and the inquiry fell outside the Commission's statutory jurisdiction.
